steps.title: Perimeter of the squares
steps.content:
One square with side length $r$ has perimeter $4r$.
$$3 \times 4r = 12r$$

steps.title: Perimeter of the triangles and total
steps.content:
One equilateral triangle with side length $t$ has perimeter $3t$.
$$6 \times 3t = 18t$$
Adding the squares’ perimeter: $$12r + 18t = 270$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
